macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Inflation Triples in Latest CPI Report Amid Iran War Gas Price Spike
items.summary: The Consumer Price Index showed inflation tripled last month, driven by a massive spike in gas prices linked to the Iran war, with pump prices over 21% higher and average weekly earnings falling nearly 1%. The White House calls it a short-term disruption dependent on the shaky ceasefire and Strait of Hormuz reopening.[1]

items.title: Tenth District Energy Activity Unchanged Despite Higher Oil Prices
items.summary: Q1 energy survey shows steady drilling and business activity in the Tenth District, with revenues and profits at highest since Q2 2022 due to elevated oil prices, but firms expect limited drilling increases ahead needing $79/barrel WTI for substantial growth.[2]

items.title: Markets Rally on Middle East Ceasefire but Remain Fragile
items.summary: A two-week US-Iran ceasefire sparked a relief rally with narrowing credit spreads, equity gains, and lower rate expectations, though gains partially reversed amid ceasefire cracks; analysts eye 'buy-on-peace' return to pre-war valuations amid resilient global economy and tech productivity boosts.[4]

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum Rally on Softer-Than-Expected Inflation Data
items.summary: Bitcoin surged to $73,111 and Ethereum reached $2,250 following the release of March inflation data showing core inflation increased just 0.2% for the month. The rally was also boosted by optimism around US-Iran ceasefire talks, with analysts suggesting potential Federal Reserve rate cuts could benefit risk assets like cryptocurrency.

items.title: Trump-Backed WLFI Token Plummets Over 12% Amid Liquidity Crisis
items.summary: World Liberty Financial's WLFI token dropped approximately 14.5% after reports revealed the project had pledged 5 billion WLFI tokens as collateral to borrow $75 million in stablecoins, effectively draining a lending pool and preventing other depositors from withdrawing funds. The crisis deepened when the team quietly scrapped a proposal to unlock $427 million worth of tokens.

items.title: Bittensor Faces Governance Crisis as Major Subnet Operator Exits
items.summary: Bittensor (TAO) experienced a sharp price drop following a governance crisis triggered by the exit of major subnet operator Covenant AI. The incident highlights ongoing concerns about altcoin stability and project management.

items.title: Bitcoin Accumulation Deepens as Market Shows Mixed Signals
items.summary: Bitcoin accumulation is intensifying with long-term conviction building, raising the likelihood of a bull run. However, analysts predict Bitcoin's market bottom won't occur until late 2026, with potential cycle lows expected around $55,000-$60,000.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic Announces Mythos AI Model but Withholds Release Over Cybersecurity Risks
items.summary: Anthropic revealed its advanced AI model Mythos, capable of identifying critical software flaws, but is not releasing it publicly due to safety concerns and potential exploitation. The announcement prompted urgent meetings by Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Fed Chair Jerome Powell with Wall Street leaders on heightened cyber risks.[1][4]

items.title: Google Releases Gemma 4 Family of Open-Source Models
items.summary: Google launched the Gemma 4 family of open-source AI models, highlighted as a key development in accessible AI tools for developers and researchers.[2]

items.title: Meta Unveils Muse Spark Multimodal AI Models
items.summary: Meta introduced Muse Spark, new multimodal models focused on personal-agent capabilities, leading benchmarks in coding and visual reasoning alongside other launches.[2][3]
items.source: AI News YouTube / The AI Daily Brief

items.title: Z.ai Open-Sources GLM 5.1, Tops Coding Benchmarks
items.summary: Z.ai released GLM 5.1 open-source model, achieving top scores on SweetBench Pro (58.4), surpassing GPT 5.4 and Opus 4.6, signaling narrowing US-China AI gap.[3]

items.title: Anthropic Acquires Coefficient Bio and Updates Claude Tools
items.summary: Anthropic acquired biotech firm Coefficient Bio and rolled out updates including Claude Code, Microsoft 365 connector, and restrictions on third-party Claude usage.[2]

items.title: OpenAI Plans Staggered Rollout of New Model for Cybersecurity Reasons
items.summary: OpenAI announced a delayed release of its latest model due to similar cybersecurity risks as Anthropic's Mythos, amid industry-wide safety concerns.[3]

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Xbox Game Pass Confirms June 2025 Wave 1 Lineup
items.summary: Microsoft has unveiled the full list of games coming to Xbox Game Pass in the first wave of June 2025 ahead of the Xbox Games Showcase.

items.title: No More Heroes Developer Announces New Ultraviolent Sci-Fi Game
items.summary: Suda51 and Grasshopper Manufacture reveal their latest over-the-top action game combining No More Heroes and Dead Rising vibes.

items.title: Helldivers 2 Reveals ‘Force of Law’ Warbond
items.summary: Helldivers 2 surprises fans with a new Force of Law Warbond coming soon to the cooperative shooter.

items.title: Xbox Schedules Summer Showcase and Gears of War: E-Day Direct for June 7
items.summary: Xbox announces its summer gaming showcase along with a dedicated Gears of War: E-Day Direct on June 7.

items.title: Immersive Sim Heist Game Thick As Thieves Shifts to Single-Player and Co-Op
items.summary: Otherside Entertainment abandons PvPvE mode in its promising immersive sim heist game to focus solely on single-player and co-op.

items.title: First Three Resident Evil Titles and Breath of Fire IV Now on Steam
items.summary: The original versions of Resident Evil 1-3 and Breath of Fire IV are now available on PC via Steam.

items.title: Epic Games Announces Major Changes to Fortnite's V-Bucks Impacting Xbox Users
items.summary: Epic Games reveals changes to Fortnite's V-Bucks system that directly affect Xbox players.

items.title: GTA 6 Insider Teases Major New Feature That Could Produce Millionaires
items.summary: A GTA 6 insider claims a key feature will create millionaires and hints at significant details.

items.title: Project Helix Price Tag Leaks as Xbox Fans React to Next Console Cost
items.summary: Leaks reveal the high price tag for Xbox's Project Helix console, sparking concern among fans.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
